properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.course: Introduction to Historic Preservation, University of Chicago

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Avondale Park Fieldhouse
properties.description: Neoclassical/colonial revival fieldhouse built 1930 by architect Clarence Hatzfield. Reduced by Kennedy Expressway construction in 1950s; $2.86M renovation in 2020.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Frank W. Reilly Elementary School
properties.description: Early 20th century school serving successive immigrant communities. First Chicago Public School to install solar panels (1999). Now 93% Latinx population.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Shoemaker Lofts (Former Florsheim Shoe Co.)
properties.description: Chicago Landmark (2006). Former industrial shoe factory adaptively reused as residential condominiums. Name preserves memory of the Florsheim Shoe Company.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: St. Joseph Village of Chicago
properties.description: Founded 1898 by the Franciscan Sisters of Chicago as the city's first nursing home. Current building established 2006 — preservation through mission rather than fabric.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Ballet Folklorico de Chicago
properties.description: Established 2018. Largest non-profit Mexican Ballet Folklorico institution in the Midwest. Serves 400+ students. A 21st century Latino institutional anchor adjacent to a century-old Polish Catholic institution.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Avondale Bowl
properties.description: Vintage 8-lane bowling alley adaptively reused as bar and event space. Exemplifies preservation of leisure and social fabric while generating modern economic benefit.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Royal Discount Center
properties.description: Longtime Milwaukee Avenue discount retailer serving successive immigrant communities. Trilingual signage reflects demographic adaptation while maintaining original commercial function.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Kurowski Sausage Shop & Rich's Bakery
properties.description: Established 1997. Carries on the tradition of the original Kurowski Sausage (est. 1945, closed early 2000s). Traditional Polish foods — sausages, baked goods, paczki — in a mixed-use storefront.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: St. Hyacinth Basilica
properties.description: Polish Cathedral, 1917–1921. Renaissance/Baroque Revival by Worthman and Steinach. Three-towered facade, 3000 sq ft copper dome, 175+ relics, ornate murals. Now offers services in English, Polish, and Spanish.

properties.author: Aurian Azghandi
properties.name: Kosciuszko Park Fieldhouse
properties.description: Tudor Revival fieldhouse, 1916, by Schwartz and Bowes. 9.01 acres. Added to National Register of Historic Places October 16, 2013. Named after Polish hero Thadeuz Kosciuszko.
